commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: May SRW wheat $5.74/bu (Apr 9 close). Pre-war $5.62. Eased from $6.07 Apr 8 on ceasefire. Fertilizer crisis remains long-term driver.

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: May corn $4.44/bu (Apr 9 close, down 3.25c). Pre-war $4.31. Easing on ceasefire hopes. USDA WASDE report Apr 9.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: May soybeans $11.65/bu (Apr 9 close, up 3.25c). Pre-war $10.12. India and Brazil 2026 crop yields expected lower.
commodities.source: Brownfield Ag News / CME March 20 closing

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: Sugar #11 (ICE) at $0.224/lb (April 2). Prices reversing earlier decline as shipping costs and diesel surcharges ripple through global supply chains. Hormuz closure driving up transport costs even for non-Gulf commodities. Sugar now modestly above pre-war levels after lagging other commodities.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Vegetable oil price index at highest since June 2022. Palm oil (CPO June) RM 4,861/tonne. Indonesia CPO ref $989.63/MT.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Global shipping costs and fuel surcharges rising sharply. Container rates up 85% on key routes. Coffee surged 6% on supply disruption fears.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

groceryImpact.note: USDA projects 3.1% food-at-home inflation in 2026. Wolfe Research estimates war could add ~2 additional percentage points. Fertilizer crisis (urea +40%, ammonia +41%) threatening Northern Hemisphere planting season.

fertilizer.detail: Approximately one-third of globally traded fertilizer transits the Strait of Hormuz. Urea surged to $590/MT (+19% in one week, April 6). Middle East urea now $700+/tonne (+40%). Ammonia prices up 41% YoY. 25% of US farmers have NOT purchased fertilizer for planting season. Wolfe Research estimates food-at-home inflation could rise ~2 percentage points. If conflict lasts >40 days, farmers may reduce plantings, affecting food supply through end of 2026.

context: Day 42 (Ceasefire Day 3): Grain prices easing slightly on ceasefire — wheat $5.74, corn $4.44. Soybeans still elevated at $11.65. FAO Vegetable Oil Index at 183.1 (highest since Jun 2022). Urea fertilizer +40%. Rice supply chains through Gulf disrupted. USDA April WASDE released.
